name: Argo
summary: Argo is an unincorporated Walker County community along U.S. Route 78 northwest of Sumiton, recognized as a populated place and connected to southeastern Walker County transportation, rural settlement, and nearby coal-era communities.
evidence_basis: Included because the existing Walker County Almanac article contains coal, mining, Warrior Coal Field, railroad, or mine-company context.

name: Barney
summary: Barney is a historic unincorporated Walker County community in the Mulberry Fork region, associated with rural settlement, coal-era development, Union Grove Baptist Church, Barney Cemetery, and nearby communities including Cordova, Dovertown, and Yerkwood.
evidence_basis: Included because the existing Walker County Almanac article contains coal, mining, Warrior Coal Field, railroad, or mine-company context.

name: Burnwell
summary: Burnwell is an unincorporated Walker County community on the southern border of Dora. It developed as part of the county's coal-mining belt, had a post office from 1910 to 2010, and retains its own ZIP code, 35038.
evidence_basis: Included because the existing Walker County Almanac article contains coal, mining, Warrior Coal Field, railroad, or mine-company context.

name: Carbon Hill
summary: Carbon Hill is a Walker County city whose history is deeply tied to coal mining, railroad development, company-town growth, fires, storms, and the Warrior Coal Field.
evidence_basis: Included because the existing Walker County Almanac article contains coal, mining, Warrior Coal Field, railroad, or mine-company context.

name: Coal Valley
summary: Coal Valley is a historic Walker County coal-mining community associated with the Corona mining district, Corona Coal Company operations, rail transportation, and the broader Warrior Coal Field.
evidence_basis: Included because the existing Walker County Almanac article contains coal, mining, Warrior Coal Field, railroad, or mine-company context.

name: Cordova
summary: Cordova is a Walker County city on the Mulberry Fork whose history includes early settlement as Dent, coal mining, railroad growth, textile manufacturing, historic downtown development, and major tornado recovery.
evidence_basis: Included because the existing Walker County Almanac article contains coal, mining, Warrior Coal Field, railroad, or mine-company context.

name: Corinth
summary: Corinth is a historic unincorporated Walker County community tied to rural settlement, churches, agriculture, local roads, and the nearby coal-era economy of surrounding communities.
evidence_basis: Included because the existing Walker County Almanac article contains coal, mining, Warrior Coal Field, railroad, or mine-company context.

name: Corona
summary: Corona is an unincorporated Walker County coal-mining community west of Oakman on Alabama State Route 18. Founded in the 1880s after the Corona Coal Company opened mines there, Corona became one of the county's foundational coal-and-rail communities.
evidence_basis: Included because the existing Walker County Almanac article contains coal, mining, Warrior Coal Field, railroad, or mine-company context.

name: Dogtown
summary: Dogtown is an unincorporated Walker County community known for rural settlement, nearby abandoned coal mines, school history, and its association with the family of federal judge Frank M. Johnson Jr.
evidence_basis: Included because the existing Walker County Almanac article contains coal, mining, Warrior Coal Field, railroad, or mine-company context.

name: Dora
summary: Dora is a Walker County city whose history is tied to coal mining, railroad development, early settlement around Sharon, and its role as an important southeastern Walker County community.
evidence_basis: Included because the existing Walker County Almanac article contains coal, mining, Warrior Coal Field, railroad, or mine-company context.

name: Dovertown
summary: Dovertown is an unincorporated Walker County community that developed around the Birmingham Forest Products plywood plant in the Bough Bend area southeast of Cordova.
evidence_basis: Included because the existing Walker County Almanac article contains coal, mining, Warrior Coal Field, railroad, or mine-company context.

name: Empire
summary: Empire is a historic coal-mining community in eastern Walker County, north of Sumiton, built around the Empire Coal Company and the Empire Mine. Its location between Little Creek and Old Town Creek, plus its railroad spur connection, made it part of the county's larger coal and transportation network.
evidence_basis: Included because the existing Walker County Almanac article contains coal, mining, Warrior Coal Field, railroad, or mine-company context.

name: Gorgas
summary: Gorgas is a Walker and Tuscaloosa County settlement formerly known as Camp Ground, later associated with Gorgas High School, the Gorgas post office, and Alabama Power's historic Gorgas steam-electric plant on the Warrior River.

name: Jasper

name: Labuco

name: Nauvoo
summary: Nauvoo is a small Walker County town near the Winston County line, rooted in the earlier Blackwell's Crossing and Ingle Mills settlements. Its growth accelerated when the Northern Alabama Railway reached the area in 1888, opening coal, timber, cotton, and local trade connections that shaped the town's historic identity.
evidence_basis: Included because the existing Walker County Almanac article contains coal, mining, Warrior Coal Field, railroad, or mine-company context.

name: Quinton
summary: Quinton is an unincorporated community in southeastern Walker County, Alabama. Located about 4.2 miles south-southeast of Dora, the community developed as part of Walker County's coal-producing region and remains an important rural community served by its own post office and ZIP code.

name: Sumiton
summary: Sumiton is a Walker County and Jefferson County city on the eastern edge of Walker County. Its history is tied to post-Civil War mining, railroad access, high-ground settlement, education, and its later role as a commercial gateway between Walker County and the Birmingham area.

name: Townley
summary: Townley is an unincorporated Walker County community west of Jasper. It was once an incorporated coal-mining town, grew rapidly during the early twentieth century, and later lost municipal status after the decline of nearby mining operations.
